summary: Lassa fever transmission is ongoing in Nigeria. Avoid contact with rats and rat-contaminated food or surfaces. Seek care immediately if you develop fever, sore throat, and muscle pain.
Cholera outbreaks are reported across several Nigerian states. Drink only boiled or treated water, wash hands with soap, and avoid open defecation. Seek care immediately for profuse watery diarrhea.
Mpox cases continue to be reported in Nigeria. Avoid close contact with people showing unexplained rashes or blisters. Report suspected cases to the nearest health facility.

Meningitis season is active in the meningitis belt states (December–June). Vaccination is available at government health facilities. Seek emergency care for sudden severe headache, stiff neck, and high fever.
Malaria remains endemic across Nigeria with peak transmission during rainy season. Use insecticide-treated bed nets, eliminate stagnant water, and seek testing for any fever lasting more than 2 days.

WHO maintains a regional watch for Nipah virus spillover events in West Africa. Avoid contact with bats and raw date palm sap. Report any cluster of unexplained encephalitis cases to health authorities.
